description: SQL Injection occurs when user-supplied data is concatenated directly into a SQL query string, allowing an attacker to alter the query's logic.
fix_description: Replace all string-interpolated SQL queries with parameterised queries. User input must be passed as a separate argument tuple, never embedded in the query string.
patch_steps: Identify every call to db.execute() (or cursor.execute()) that uses f-strings, %-formatting, or string concatenation.
Replace the query string with a plain string using '?' (SQLite) or '%s' (PostgreSQL/MySQL) placeholders.
Pass the user-controlled values as a second argument tuple to execute().
Run the test suite to confirm the query still returns the correct data.
verification_rules: No f-strings inside any execute() call
No '+' string concatenation inside any execute() call
All user-controlled values supplied as a parameters tuple
references: CWE-89: Improper Neutralization of Special Elements used in an SQL Command
